---
title: "Week 11: Apr 6 - Apr 12, 2026"
subtitle: "Unit IV-V: Spring Data, Security, and Spring Boot"
---

## Week Overview

This week covers Spring Data JPA, Spring Security, and introduces Spring Boot as a modern way to build Spring applications.

**Course Outcome:** CO-4, CO-5 (K4-K5 - Analysis & Synthesis)  
**Duration:** 1 week (4 lectures + 1 lab)  
**Lab:** Spring Boot Application Development

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
## Quick Practice Quizzes (Lectures 33-36)
Use the [Quiz Hub](/quiz/index.qmd) or jump directly to:

- [Lecture 33 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-33-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 34 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-34-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 35 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-35-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 36 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-36-quiz.qmd)
:::

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
This week uses upcoming lecture decks. Use the [Lectures Index](/lectures/index.qmd) for all available slides.
:::

---

## Lecture 33: Spring Core Basics & Dependency Injection {#lecture-33}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-33-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-33.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-33.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### What is Spring?
- Lightweight Java application framework for enterprise apps
- Provides IoC container, DI, AOP, and MVC
- Removes tight coupling between classes
- Large ecosystem: Spring Core, Boot, Data, Security, Cloud

### Inversion of Control (IoC)
- Framework controls object creation — not your code
- Spring IoC container reads configuration and wires beans
- Key container: `ApplicationContext`

### Dependency Injection Types

| Type | How | When to Use |
|---|---|---|
| Constructor | `@Autowired` on constructor | Preferred — explicit, immutable |
| Setter | `@Autowired` on setter method | Optional dependencies |
| Field | `@Autowired` on field | Avoid — not testable |

### Spring Configuration Styles
- **XML**: `<bean>` declarations in XML — legacy projects
- **Java Config**: `@Configuration` + `@Bean` — full control
- **Annotations**: `@Component` + `@Autowired` — modern projects

---

## Lecture 34: Design Patterns: Factory & Strategy {#lecture-34}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-34-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-34.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-34.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### Design Pattern Categories

| Category | Purpose | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| Creational | Object creation | Factory, Singleton, Builder |
| Structural | Object composition | Adapter, Decorator, Proxy |
| Behavioral | Object communication | Strategy, Observer, Template |

### Factory Pattern
- Intent: centralise object creation behind a factory method
- Caller uses only the interface — not the concrete class
- Spring's `BeanFactory` and `ApplicationContext` implement this pattern

```java
// Factory method
public static Notification create(String type) {
  return switch (type) {
    case "email" -> new EmailNotification();
    case "sms"   -> new SMSNotification();
    default      -> new PushNotification();
  };
}
```

### Strategy Pattern
- Intent: swap algorithms/behaviours at runtime via a shared interface
- Context holds a reference to the strategy — can be changed
- Spring DI naturally implements Strategy via `@Autowired` + `@Qualifier`

```java
public interface SortStrategy { void sort(int[] data); }
class QuickSort  implements SortStrategy { ... }
class MergeSort  implements SortStrategy { ... }

public class Sorter {
  private SortStrategy strategy;
  public Sorter(SortStrategy s) { this.strategy = s; }
  public void sort(int[] data) { strategy.sort(data); }
}
```

---

## Lecture 35: Spring IoC, Bean Scopes & Life Cycle Callbacks {#lecture-35}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-35-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-35.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-35.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### Bean Scopes

| Scope | Instances | Lifetime |
|---|---|---|
| `singleton` | One per container | Until container shuts down |
| `prototype` | New per `getBean()` | Caller managed |
| `request` | One per HTTP request | Until response sent |
| `session` | One per HTTP session | Until session expires |
| `application` | One per `ServletContext` | Until app stops |

### Bean Lifecycle
Instantiate → Inject Dependencies → `@PostConstruct` / `afterPropertiesSet` / init-method → Bean Ready → `@PreDestroy` / `destroy` / destroy-method

### Lifecycle Callback Approaches
```java
@Component
public class DatabasePool {

  @PostConstruct
  public void init()    { /* open connections  */ }

  @PreDestroy
  public void cleanup() { /* close connections */ }
}
```

- `@PostConstruct` / `@PreDestroy` — preferred (no Spring API coupling)
- `InitializingBean` / `DisposableBean` interfaces — programmatic
- `@Bean(initMethod, destroyMethod)` — for third-party classes

---

## Lecture 36: AOP, Autowiring, Annotations, Bean Configuration Styles {#lecture-36}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-36-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-36.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-36.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::


### AOP Terminology

| Term | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Aspect | Module holding cross-cutting logic |
| Advice | The code that runs (Before/After/Around) |
| Pointcut | Rule selecting which methods to target |
| Join Point | Specific method execution point |
| Weaving | Applying aspect to target classes |

### Logging Aspect Example
```java
@Aspect @Component
public class LoggingAspect {
  @Pointcut("execution(* com.example.service.*.*(..))")
  public void serviceLayer() {}

  @Before("serviceLayer()")
  public void logBefore(JoinPoint jp) {
    System.out.println("Entering: " + jp.getSignature().getName());
  }

  @Around("serviceLayer()")
  public Object measureTime(ProceedingJoinPoint pjp) throws Throwable {
    long start = System.currentTimeMillis();
    Object res = pjp.proceed();
    System.out.println(pjp.getSignature() + " took " +
      (System.currentTimeMillis() - start) + "ms");
    return res;
  }
}
```

### Autowiring and Qualifiers
- `@Autowired` — inject matching bean by type
- `@Primary` — default bean when multiple candidates exist
- `@Qualifier("beanName")` — select specific bean by name

### Spring Stereotype Annotations
- `@Component` — generic managed bean
- `@Service` — business logic layer
- `@Repository` — data access layer (with exception translation)
- `@Controller` / `@RestController` — web MVC layer

### Configuration Style Summary

| Style | Key Annotation | Best For |
|---|---|---|
| XML | `<bean>` | Legacy projects |
| Java Config | `@Configuration` + `@Bean` | Infrastructure beans |
| Annotation | `@Component` + `@Autowired` | Application classes |

---

## Lab 9: Spring Boot Application

### Objectives
- Create Spring Boot REST API
- Implement JPA repositories
- Add security
- Deploy application

### Activities
1. Create Spring Boot project
2. Configure database connection
3. Create User entity and repository
4. Build REST API endpoints
5. Add basic security
6. Test endpoints with Postman

### Deliverables
- Working Spring Boot application
- Complete CRUD REST API
- Database integration
- Running on localhost

---

## Key Takeaways

✅ Spring IoC container manages object creation and wiring  
✅ Constructor injection is preferred for mandatory dependencies  
✅ Factory pattern centralises creation; Strategy pattern swaps behaviour  
✅ Bean scopes control how many instances Spring creates  
✅ Lifecycle callbacks (`@PostConstruct`, `@PreDestroy`) handle setup and cleanup  
✅ AOP separates cross-cutting concerns from business logic  
✅ Stereotype annotations (`@Service`, `@Repository`) organise application layers

---

## Next Week

Unit V final topics:
- REST API best practices
- Testing strategies
- Deployment and production
